mapping.a_element: critical/quasi-extremal parameter separating regular geometries from configurations with integrable singularities
mapping.b_element: critical coupling separating globally regular electrostatic solutions from singular ones

brief_justification: Paper A engineers a two-parameter {M,a} family of regular axisymmetric black-hole metrics interpolating to Kerr (a gravitational metric-construction problem), whereas paper B integrates a quartic nonlinear-electrodynamics gauge equation in a fixed AdS4 geometry and computes holographic fermion Green's/spectral functions; there is no shared governing equation or role-for-role dynamical mapping, only the generic notion of a critical parameter demarcating regular solutions and shared relativistic-physics vocabulary.
